name: MongoDB Lens MCP Server
what_it_does: Community MCP server for MongoDB — enabling AI agents to query, insert, update, and delete documents, manage collections and indexes, run aggregation pipelines, and inspect database schemas in MongoDB or MongoDB Atlas.
use_cases: Querying MongoDB collections with complex filters from agent workflows
Running aggregation pipelines for data analysis via agents
Inserting and updating documents from agent-driven workflows
Schema inspection and collection stats for context-aware agents
Index management and query performance analysis
not_for: MongoDB Atlas management plane (cluster creation, user management)
High-frequency real-time writes requiring connection pooling
best_when: An agent needs direct MongoDB data access — querying, updating, or analyzing documents and collections in a MongoDB database.
avoid_when: You need cluster management (use MongoDB Atlas API); use a SQL database (use Postgres/MySQL MCP).

auth.notes: MongoDB connection string with credentials. Supports all MongoDB auth mechanisms. MongoDB Atlas connection string is the typical configuration.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source MCP; MongoDB Atlas free tier: 512MB shared cluster
pricing.paid_tiers: MongoDB Atlas paid tiers from $57/month dedicated
pricing.notes: MCP server is free open source. MongoDB Atlas has a free tier. Self-hosted MongoDB is free.

performance.rate_limits: No explicit limits; dependent on MongoDB server configuration

known_agent_gotchas: Connection string contains credentials — must be passed securely, never hardcoded
MongoDB schema is flexible but agents should inspect collection structure before querying
Aggregation pipelines can be complex — agents may construct invalid pipelines
Large result sets need cursor pagination — always add limit to queries
Community MCP — not official MongoDB; maintenance depends on community
ObjectId type requires special handling in queries — string vs ObjectId mismatch common
Transactions require replica set or sharded cluster — not available on standalone
agent_readiness.security_notes: TLS available for MongoDB connections (configure in connection string). Connection string with credentials requires secure storage. MongoDB Atlas enforces TLS. Role-based access control in MongoDB provides collection-level security.

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS available but must be explicitly configured. RBAC at role/collection level. Connection string secret management is operator responsibility. Community MCP.
